title: Trees (BST, AVL, Red-Black)
description: Explore Binary Search Trees (BST), self-balancing trees (AVL, Red-Black), and spatial partitioning trees.
learningObjectives: Describe self-balancing operations (rotations in AVL/Red-Black trees)
Explain spatial trees (Quadtrees, Octrees) used in collision detection

sections.title: Tree Hierarchies and Self-Balancing
sections.content: Trees organize hierarchical data. A Binary Search Tree (BST) stores smaller values in the left subtree and larger in the right, enabling O(log N) searches. If elements are inserted sequentially (e.g. 1, 2, 3), the tree degenerates into a linear linked list (O(N)). Self-balancing trees (AVL, Red-Black) use rotations to keep height balanced. Game engines use spatial trees (like Quadtrees for 2D, Octrees for 3D) to partition space and rapidly prune objects outside the camera frustum.

sections.title: Basic BST Node Traversal
